Kris Daly, Paralegal

Kris Daly joined the Law Offices of Edward A. Smith in 2025 as a legal assistant. After working for doctor's offices for years, she began working in the legal field as a secretary for a criminal attorney.  Since then she worked in insurance defense, personal injury, employment law, and Tribal Law.  Her medical background has given her knowledge and an advantage regarding medical facilities, medical care and understanding of injuries and treatment options.  Insurance defense gave her an understanding of the opposition in personal injury cases and insight into the thought patterns and procedures of defense counsel.  Due to her inclination to help people in need, she has continued to work in personal injury in our office.  In her time off, Kris prefers to be outdoors, hiking, camping, snowshoeing, traveling and scuba diving.

title: Add content to Wrongful death page
description:
Seeking help add this to the content they provided previously for their Wrongful death page.
https://autoaccident.com/sacramento-wrongful-death-lawyer/

Here is the previous task they asked for: Sacramento Wrongful Death Lawyer update - J302 Edward A Smith Law Offices

  • Please review and advise on this piece of content. If we can use it, please rewrite this content, as Ed was flagged for plagiarism in Copyscape. 
  • Please also make sure that the phone numbers are clickable.
California Wrongful Death Law Overview
Losing a loved one to someone else’s negligence is one of the hardest things a family can endure. In Sacramento, wrongful death laws give surviving relatives a path to seek accountability and financial support when a preventable tragedy occurs.

A wrongful death claim in California is a civil action brought when a person’s death is caused by the wrongful act or negligence of another. In Sacramento, these cases frequently stem from incidents such as car accidents on I-5 or Highway 99 (/car-accidents/), motorcycle crashes on busy roads like J Street or Folsom Boulevard (/motorcycle-accidents/), truck collisions on I-80 (/truck-accidents/), dangerous property conditions, defective products, or medical malpractice at local hospitals such as UC Davis Medical Center, Sutter General, or Mercy San Juan.

Who Can File a Wrongful Death Claim in California?
Under California law (Code of Civil Procedure §377.60), the following people are typically allowed to bring a wrongful death lawsuit:
    •    The spouse or domestic partner of the deceased
    •    The children of the deceased
    •    Stepchildren or dependent minors living in the household
    •    If none of the above survive, other relatives who would inherit under California’s intestate succession laws

Types of Damages Available
Families may be able to recover compensation for both economic and non-economic losses, such as:
    •    Funeral and burial expenses in Sacramento or surrounding counties
    •    Loss of the deceased’s financial support and income
    •    The value of household services the loved one would have provided
    •    Loss of love, companionship, guidance, and emotional support

Statute of Limitations
Most wrongful death lawsuits in California must be filed within two years from the date of death. Families in Sacramento should not wait to take action — missing this deadline can prevent you from pursuing justice. A knowledgeable Sacramento wrongful death lawyer (/wrongful-death/) can make sure your claim is filed properly and on time.

Why Local Legal Help Matters
Wrongful death cases often involve major insurance carriers and defense attorneys who try to minimize payouts. Having an experienced Sacramento attorney who knows the local courts, judges, and insurance tactics can make a powerful difference in protecting your family’s future.

⸻

We’re Here for Your Family
If your family has lost a loved one because of negligence, we are here to help you find answers and pursue justice. Call us today at (916) 921-6400 or (800) 404-5400 for a free, compassionate consultation with a Sacramento wrongful death attorney. 


Thanks much.

title: Wrongful death FAQs
description:
Please review the provided FAQs for the wrongful death page. Please let the team know to use their judgment and only post what they think will be helpful. 

From the client:
FAQs About Who Can File a Wrongful Death Claim in California

Can siblings bring a wrongful death claim in California?
In most cases, siblings do not have standing to file unless they were financially dependent on the person who passed away. 

Priority is given to spouses, children, and parents.

What if the deceased was unmarried with no children?
If there is no surviving spouse or child, the parents may bring the claim. If parents are no longer living, other dependents such as stepchildren or financially supported relatives may have rights.

Can multiple family members file separate claims?
No. California law requires all eligible heirs to join together in a single wrongful death action. This ensures fairness and prevents multiple lawsuits over the same loss.

What if we’re unsure who can file in our family?
Every situation is unique. An experienced wrongful death lawyer can review your family’s circumstances and explain who has the legal right to bring a claim.

Thanks much

title: Sacramento Wrongful Death Lawyer update
description:
Seeking help in updating the start of the wrongful death page. 
https://autoaccident.com/sacramento-wrongful-death-lawyer/

I have included the one they sent please review the one they sent and make any suggestions needed to update the page. 

 
Wrongful Death in Sacramento
Losing a loved one in an unexpected accident is one of the hardest experiences a family can endure. Beyond the grief, there are often unanswered questions, financial strain, and the feeling that life will never be the same. While no legal action can replace what was lost, a wrongful death claim can bring accountability and provide the resources a family needs to begin rebuilding.

For more than 40 years, our Sacramento wrongful death lawyers have stood beside families facing these painful circumstances. We understand the emotional weight of these cases and approach them with compassion, respect, and the determination to seek justice. Whether the loss was caused by a car crash, medical negligence, or another act of carelessness, we are here to protect your rights and fight for the support your family deserves.


When you’re ready to talk, we’re here to help. Call us at (916) 921-6400.


A Sacramento Family’s Story
After a tragic crash on Highway 99 took the life of a young father, his family was left grieving and unsure how to manage mounting bills. The insurance company offered a quick settlement that would not have covered even a fraction of their long-term needs. Our wrongful death team stepped in, gathered evidence, and worked with experts to fully value the family’s loss — from lost income to the irreplaceable role of a husband and parent. In the end, we secured a settlement that provided financial stability and, just as importantly, a sense of justice.

Thanks much

title: Bicycle accident update
description:
Hi Dorin,

This is somehow similar to the request for their Car accident page. The client sent an update they want to apply to the 1st paragraph from this page: https://autoaccident.com/bicycle-accidents/

If ever there will be SEO implications please let us know so we can inform their Account Manager.


Here is the updated text they want to use:

Bicycle Accidents in Sacramento

Sacramento is a city built for cyclists — from the American River Bike Trail to the busy streets of Midtown and Downtown. Every day, thousands of riders share the road with cars, trucks, and buses. Unfortunately, this vibrant bike culture also means a higher risk of serious collisions. The California Office of Traffic Safety ranks Sacramento among the top counties for bicycle crashes, with distracted drivers, unsafe lane changes, and dooring accidents leading the list.

At AutoAccident.com, we’ve represented injured cyclists across Sacramento for more than 40 years. We understand the unique challenges riders face — from traumatic injuries to insurance companies that try to shift the blame. Our mission is simple: protect Sacramento’s cycling community and fight for the compensation injured riders need to heal and move forward.

Thanks much

At AutoAccident.com, we’ve built our reputation on one promise: we fight for injured people and their families, and you never pay unless we win. 
 
A serious crash can turn life upside down—medical bills, time away from work, and relentless calls from insurance companies can leave you feeling overwhelmed. 
 
For decades, we’ve helped Sacramento residents find their footing after collisions. 
 
Whether it happened on I-5, Highway 99, Business 80, or the Capital City Freeway near downtown, we know the local roads and the patterns of crashes that occur on them. 
 
With our experience and the millions in verdicts and settlements obtained for past clients, we’re prepared to protect your rights and pursue the compensation 
 
Call us now for free, friendly advice at 916-921-6400 or
(800) 404-5400 

Thanks much.

title: 10DLC Discussion
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag
description:
Dear Clixsy:

We're trying to work with our phone provider GoTo to get ourselves compliant with the 10DLC requirements for continuing to use SMS text messaging with our current and potential clients, specifically those who submit their phone numbers via the contact form on our site at autoaccident.com.

Are you familiar with the requirements for 10DLC compliance?  (GoTo does not make it a straightforward experience.)  They indicate that we will need the following:

The "How Can We Help" form on our website's contact page and a couple other pages needs:
  • A second check box to explicitly opt out of receiving text messages.
  • The reference in the form to "Terms" should read "Terms of Service" and should include the link to our page at https://autoaccident.com/terms-of-service/
  • The reference in the form to "Privacy Policy" should include the link to our page at https://autoaccident.com/privacy-policy/

I.e., the text "Terms of Service" and "Privacy Policy" need to specifically be within the body of the form itself, not just included lower down on the page.  That text currently in the form is not linked.

Please advise when these changes can be completed to that we can re-submit our site for approval.  We understand the deadline for this is mid-January.

Please let me know if you have any questions.  Thank you!

title: Update outdated info on this page
description:
, can you update the copy on this page for the Bodily and Property sections as legislation was updated in January when they passed the Senate Bill 1107, known as the Protect California Drivers Act.

The client didn't have a specific direction on how to update it besides wanting us to reword the policy limits with the new legislation.

Here's some copy that Ed Smith (firm owner) sent over but Jeanette (CMO) is unsure if he created this in perplexity, so please use it as a reference only:

"If personal injury damages exceed the minimum liability insurance coverage required by California law, this may be an issue. The current minimum auto liability limits required in California include:

  • $15,000 per person for serious injury or wrongful death
  • $30,000 for all parties involved in the traffic accident
  • $5,000 property damage
However, at the beginning of 2025, California drivers will face higher minimum auto insurance liability limits due to Senate Bill 1107, also known as the Protect California Drivers Act. This legislation raises the required coverage to $30,000 for bodily injury or death per person, $60,000 per accident, and $15,000 for property damage. By 2035, these amounts will increase further, ensuring even more comprehensive coverage.

For you as a driver, these changes mean better financial protection if you’re involved in an accident, reducing the chance of paying for damages or injuries yourself. However, with higher coverage requirements, you can expect your auto insurance premiums to increase as insurance companies adjust their rates. While this might raise your monthly payments, the added protection could be crucial in serious accidents, giving you greater security and peace of mind while driving."


I highlighted the areas that are outdated according to what they sent over. Let me know if you need anything else from me.

title: Thank You Page Needed for meta Ad attribution
description:
Hi , 

Cerelia helps Jeanette, at Law Offices for Ed Smith, with their ads. Currently, she's setting up a campaign and is requesting a separate thank you page to have users directed to once they complete the form on the contact us page. Here's the  direct message: 


"If Clixsy can make this update before next Thursday, that would be ideal. Here’s the core of the request you can pass along:

We’d like users to be redirected to a separate "Thank You" page after submitting their information through the lead form here https://autoaccident.com/contact-us/. Currently, users remain on the same page, and a red message appears stating, "Thank you for your message. It has been sent." While this confirms the submission, staying on the same page limits Meta’s ability to properly track conversions.

The new page can be simple, and will come up after someone submits their info, with messaging like:
“Thanks for contacting us! We’ve received your submission and someone from our team will be in touch within 24–48 hours. If you need immediate assistance, please call us directly at 916-921-6400.”

It doesn’t need to be elaborate—just clearly distinct from the form on the Contact Us page. This redirect is key to helping Meta recognize completed actions and optimize ad performance moving forward.

Let me know if you’d prefer I reach out to Clixsy directly, or if there’s anything else you need from me"

title: Insights? Blog reported down for 7 minutes
description:
Good Morning!

Heads up, the client reported the blog site was down shy of 7 minutes. Here's the report they sent us. Please review this and let us know if a specific reason can be identified.

The specific time they received the email notification was this morning at 6:30 MST.

Thanks!

title: NightWatch Reports
description: , when Jeannette sends over the NightWatch report, what do you want us to look for and take way from them?

title: Client Approval Needed for Content?
description:
Hey and ,

Just wanted to check with you if this client needs to approve any content before we publish it?

- On site content for new pages, blogs, etc.
- GBP post content, which is typically -
     - content from a newly generated blog post on site.
     - summarized content created from an existing page on site.
     - sometimes we may create content on a topic and create a post for it.
- Press Releases

Thanks!

title: Are we using any of these phone numbers on the site?
description:
Hi, guys! We just got an email from Jeanette asking if these numbers are used anywhere on the site. I'm hoping you guys have some cool tool that'll allow you to check :) Just let me know! Thank you!

title: In Scope & have bandwidth?
description:
The client is asking if we can update this page: https://autoaccident.com/team/ to reflect the staff's order according to seniority.

Would this fall in scope and does the team have bandwidth? I would also ensure the client provides the order.

* Bonus question: Do the sliders have functionality for setting the order of the staff portrayed as well? don't judge me for asking, please 🫣
